Scenario.overview: Communications Complex The ShadowRay campaign aims to disrupt communication services by targeting organizations in the communications industry.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Web server services such as emails and databases, Shared DB or directory, Vulnerability, Authentication issues
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Phishing, Web, Vulnerability exploit, Credential compromise

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The ShadowRay campaign begins by gathering information about the target organization's communications infrastructure through open-source intelligence.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The campaign develops a phishing campaign exploiting CVE-2023-48022 in the Ray AI framework to trick employees into revealing sensitive information.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2021-42258 [CWE-22] : Microsoft Exchange Server Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
stage.description: The campaign initiates access by sending targeted phishing emails with malicious attachments exploiting CVE-2021-42258 to employees within the target organization.

Scenario.summary: The ShadowRay campaign employs a complex attack strategy involving reconnaissance, resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact to disrupt communication services within the target industry.
